mastercard.title: No Cardholder Authorization
mastercard.description: Cardholder did not authorize the transaction.

amex.title: Goods/Services Not Received
amex.description: Commonly paired when delivery cannot be validated.

discover.title: Cardholder Dispute
discover.description: Service or charge is disputed by cardholder.

mastercard.title: Cardholder Dispute
mastercard.description: Cardholder indicates expected services were not provided.

amex.title: Goods/Services Not Received
amex.description: Cardholder did not receive goods or services.

amex.title: Canceled Services
amex.description: Cardholder canceled but was billed anyway.

13.1.title: Merchandise/Services Not Received
strategyTips: Emphasize item never arrived or was inaccessible
13.1.customerStrategy: Highlight that you never received the item or service. Challenge delivery confirmation with any mismatches in address or signature, and show you contacted the merchant before disputing.
13.1.maxDisputeWindow: Typically up to 120 days depending on issuer policy.

13.2.title: Canceled Recurring Transaction
strategyTips: Demonstrate lack of usage after renewal
13.2.customerStrategy: Emphasize your cancellation attempts, any confusing or hidden policies, and show that you didn't use the service after the renewal date. Use timestamps to your advantage.
13.2.maxDisputeWindow: Typically up to 120 days depending on issuer policy.

13.3.title: Not as Described or Defective
13.3.description: Product or service materially differs from what was represented.
commonMerchantRebuttals: Customer didn't raise issues before disputing
strategyTips: Document differences between what was advertised vs received
Show early attempts to return or resolve
13.3.customerStrategy: Include side-by-side comparisons and clearly point out misrepresentation. The goal is to show that the merchant misled you and you tried resolving it first.
13.3.maxDisputeWindow: Typically up to 120 days depending on issuer policy.

10.4.title: Other Fraud—Card-Absent Environment
strategyTips: Note IP address inconsistency if any
Show card was stolen, compromised, or used without your knowledge
Indicate lack of past relationship with merchant
10.4.customerStrategy: Stress that this charge was unauthorized, and you didn’t benefit from it. Include fraud report or any alerts issued by your bank.
10.4.maxDisputeWindow: Typically up to 120 days depending on issuer policy.

4853.title: Cardholder Dispute
4853.customerStrategy: Highlight that you followed their instructions and still got charged. Include timestamps, show no usage after the cancellation, and challenge how clear the terms were.
4853.maxDisputeWindow: Typically up to 120 days depending on issuer policy.

4837.title: No Cardholder Authorization
commonMerchantRebuttals: Product was custom or final sale
strategyTips: Emphasize misleading or vague return policies
4837.customerStrategy: Argue that the item wasn’t what was promised or was defective. Support this with photos, and show that you tried to contact the seller or return the item.
4837.maxDisputeWindow: Typically up to 120 days depending on issuer policy.

C08.title: Goods/Services Not Received
C08.description: Goods/services not received or canceled as promised.
C08.customerStrategy: Show that the merchant’s promise wasn’t kept and their policy was confusing or misrepresented. Include screenshots of policies and attempts to get help or cancel.
C08.maxDisputeWindow: Typically up to 120 days depending on issuer policy.

RG.title: Cardholder Dispute
strategyTips: Prove service was never used or was inaccessible
Show lack of consent to terms
Dispute any auto-renewals with unclear disclosures
RG.customerStrategy: Make the case that you didn’t access or benefit from the service, and show the merchant made it unclear or hard to cancel or opt out.
RG.maxDisputeWindow: Typically up to 120 days depending on issuer policy.

submissionNotes: Include dispute reference number from online claim.
Attach timeline and merchant communications in chronological order.

contact.mailingAddress: P.O. Box 30285, Salt Lake City, UT 84130
submissionNotes: Upload evidence as PDF when possible.
Cite the charge date and posted date if both are visible.
